assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSOR 1 — real_world_framing_007 (Run 2)

The behavioral feedback loop is a critical deployment concern: documented mode switching changes load dynamics and corrupts the static training environment assumptions. The threshold was not calibrated for passenger trust. Both are deployment-specific gaps. An A/B test measuring behavioral response and calibration stability is required before full deployment.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSOR 2 — real_world_framing_007 (Run 2)

F1 = 0.72 is adequate. Two deployment concerns: documented feedback loop from mode switching, and intuitively-set threshold. Both require empirical measurement before full-scale deployment. Controlled A/B test across stop clusters recommended.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSOR 3 — real_world_framing_007 (Run 2)

The transit authority survey data makes the feedback loop a concrete documented concern, not theoretical. The threshold is a service design gap. Both issues addressed by controlled pilot before full rollout.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS — real_world_framing_007 (Ensemble, Run 2)

Consensus across all three assessors: behavioral feedback loop (documented) and threshold calibration gap. Unanimous recommendation for A/B test pilot before full deployment.

EMPIRICAL TEST SPECIFICATION: A/B test across stop clusters with threshold variants for 60 days measuring ridership change and calibration stability.

empirical_test.condition: A/B test of threshold variants across stop clusters for 60 days.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Significant ridership decline at alerted stops alters delay patterns.
